text.title: Catalog protocol for shared deps
text.summary: Declare every cross-package external dependency once in pnpm-workspace.yaml; consume via "catalog:". Internal packages use "workspace:*".

section.title: The protocol
section.p: The pnpm catalog is a central version registry for the workspace. A `package.json` referencing `"react": "catalog:"` resolves to whatever version is declared at the workspace root. Default catalog entries use the bare `catalog:` literal; named catalogs use `catalog:<name>` for variants (e.g., a "next" catalog for canary versions).

rule.statement: External dependencies that appear in two or more workspace packages MUST be declared once under `catalog:` (or a named catalog) in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and referenced with `"<pkg>": "catalog:"` in every package.json. Internal workspace packages MUST be referenced as `"<pkg>": "workspace:*"`. Hard-coded versions (e.g., `"react": "^19.0.0"`) for shared deps are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Catalogs make version drift impossible by construction: there is exactly one place a version is defined. Without catalogs, agents adding the same dep in two packages will pick whatever range is current that day, producing duplicate trees and subtle compatibility breakages.

example.code: # pnpm-workspace.yaml packages: - 'packages/*' - 'apps/*' catalog: react: ^19.0.0 react-dom: ^19.0.0 typescript: ^5.7.0 vitest: ^3.0.0 catalogs: next: react: 19.1.0-canary
example.note: Bumping React = single line change in pnpm-workspace.yaml; every workspace updates atomically on `pnpm install`.
Two React versions, two trees, runtime breakage when shared components cross the boundary.

antipattern.description: Adding a new shared dep via `pnpm add react -F web@^19.1.2` without updating the catalog.
antipattern.reason: Bypasses the catalog and reintroduces drift. Add deps via `pnpm add react -F web` (it picks up the catalog default) or update the catalog first if a new version is needed.
